title: A/B Testing (LLM)
existing_short_definition: Running a randomized experiment where different user segments receive different model versions or prompts, measuring impact on business metrics.

title: Active Learning
existing_short_definition: A labeling strategy where the model selects the most informative examples to label next, maximizing model improvement per annotation budget.

title: AgentBench / AgentEval
existing_short_definition: Benchmarks that evaluate LLM agents on multi-step tasks in realistic environments — shells, browsers, databases, OSes — rather than single-shot Q&A.

title: AI Observability (Reprise)
existing_short_definition: The practice of monitoring LLM and ML systems in production — traces, cost, quality, safety events, drift — to ensure reliability and continuous improvement.

title: Annotation (Data Labeling)
existing_short_definition: The process of humans labeling examples — spans, classes, preferences, ratings — to create training or evaluation data.

title: Arize Phoenix
existing_short_definition: Arize's open-source ML and LLM observability platform with OpenTelemetry-based tracing, RAG evaluation, and drift detection.

title: BEIR (Retrieval Benchmark)
existing_short_definition: A zero-shot retrieval benchmark spanning 18 diverse datasets (news, bio, Wikipedia, finance, fact-checking) used to evaluate retrieval models out-of-domain.

title: BigCodeBench
existing_short_definition: A coding benchmark that evaluates LLMs on tasks requiring library usage and complex instruction following — a more realistic test than HumanEval.

title: Braintrust
existing_short_definition: A platform for LLM evaluation, dataset management, and logging that integrates evaluation into the development workflow like unit tests.

title: Canary Deployment
existing_short_definition: A rollout strategy that sends a small fraction of traffic to a new model version before full promotion, catching regressions early.

title: Chatbot Arena (LMSYS)
existing_short_definition: LMSYS's crowd-sourced pairwise LLM evaluation platform where users vote on anonymous side-by-side responses, producing an Elo leaderboard.

title: Cost per Query (CPQ)
existing_short_definition: The total LLM spend for a single user interaction — including input tokens, output tokens, reasoning tokens, tool calls, and embedding lookups.

title: Data Quality
existing_short_definition: The measurable properties that determine how useful a dataset is — accuracy, completeness, consistency, freshness, coverage, and labeling reliability.

title: DeepEval
existing_short_definition: An open-source pytest-style framework for LLM evaluation — runs metrics (hallucination, relevance, bias, toxicity) as test assertions.

title: Drift Detection
existing_short_definition: Monitoring for changes in input data distribution, labels, or model predictions over time that may degrade model performance.

title: Eval Harness (Evaluation Framework)
existing_short_definition: A framework that runs a model against a battery of test cases, scores each with metrics, and produces comparable results across models or versions.

title: Feature Store
existing_short_definition: Infrastructure that stores and serves engineered features for ML with guarantees about consistency between training and serving.

title: G-Eval
existing_short_definition: An LLM-as-judge method that uses chain-of-thought evaluation steps and form-filling to produce reliable scores for generation quality.

title: Golden Dataset (Eval Dataset)
existing_short_definition: A curated, hand-checked set of (input, expected output) pairs used as the ground truth for evaluating LLM changes — the single most valuable artifact in a production LLM app.
existing_code_example:
# Minimal golden dataset format
[
  {'input': 'What is the capital of France?', 'expected': 'Paris', 'tags': ['trivia', 'geography']},
  {'input': 'Summarize in one sentence: [long doc]', 'expected_rubric': 'contains key facts: X, Y, Z', 'tags': ['summarization']},
]

title: GPQA (Graduate-Level Google-Proof QA)
existing_short_definition: A 448-question benchmark of graduate-level science (physics, biology, chemistry) that PhDs without internet access score ~65% on — a hard reasoning test.

title: GSM8K
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's 8k-example grade-school math word-problem benchmark — the starter reasoning eval that most LLMs now saturate.

title: Helicone
existing_short_definition: An LLM observability and gateway platform focused on logging, cost tracking, caching, and rate limiting — one proxy line away from adoption.

title: HumanEval (Benchmark)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's 164-problem Python code benchmark where solutions are scored by unit-test pass rate — the canonical LLM coding eval.

title: Inference
existing_short_definition: Running an AI model to get output — as opposed to training it.

title: Langfuse
existing_short_definition: An open-source observability and evaluation platform for LLM apps — traces, prompts, eval runs, user feedback — self-hostable alternative to LangSmith.

title: LangSmith
existing_short_definition: LangChain's observability and evaluation platform for LLM applications — traces, datasets, evals, and prompt management in one tool.

title: LiveCodeBench
existing_short_definition: A continually updated coding benchmark that sources problems from LeetCode, AtCoder, and Codeforces after each model's training cutoff to avoid contamination.

title: LLM-as-Judge
existing_short_definition: Using one LLM to grade another LLM's output against a rubric — the most scalable evaluation method for tasks without clear reference answers.

title: LLM-as-Judge (Reprise)
existing_short_definition: Using a strong LLM to score or pairwise-compare other LLMs' outputs — a scalable substitute for human evaluation with well-documented biases.

title: Observability (LLM Observability)
existing_short_definition: The discipline of capturing, storing, and analyzing every LLM call in production — prompts, responses, tokens, latency, cost, user feedback — to debug and improve the system.
existing_code_example:
# Langfuse auto-instrumented OpenAI
from langfuse.openai import openai
response = openai.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    messages=[{'role':'user','content': q}],
    metadata={'user_id': user.id, 'session': sess, 'prompt_version': 'v2.1'},
)

title: Local vs Cloud AI
existing_short_definition: Local AI runs on your own hardware. Cloud AI runs on someone else's servers.

title: LongBench / RULER
existing_short_definition: Benchmarks that stress-test long-context LLMs with multi-hop QA, retrieval, summarization, and synthetic tasks up to hundreds of thousands of tokens.

title: MATH (Competition Math Benchmark)
existing_short_definition: A 12,500-problem benchmark of competition math (AMC/AIME-level) — much harder than GSM8K and still unsaturated for most models.

title: MLflow
existing_short_definition: An open-source platform for the ML lifecycle — experiment tracking, model registry, deployment — increasingly extended with LLM-specific features.

title: MMLU (Benchmark)
existing_short_definition: Massive Multitask Language Understanding — a 57-subject multiple-choice benchmark that became the standard broad-knowledge test for LLMs.

title: MMLU-Pro
existing_short_definition: A harder, less-contaminated successor to MMLU with 10-way multiple choice and harder questions, reducing test-taking-skill effects.

title: Model Registry
existing_short_definition: A versioned catalog of trained models with metadata — training run, metrics, stage (staging/prod), lineage — governing which model is deployed.

title: LLM Router / Model Routing
existing_short_definition: A pattern that dispatches each query to the most cost-effective model capable of handling it — cheap model for easy queries, expensive model only when needed.

title: Model Serving
existing_short_definition: The infrastructure that hosts trained models behind an API with low latency, high availability, and scaling properties needed for production traffic.

title: MTEB (Massive Text Embedding Benchmark)
existing_short_definition: A benchmark suite of 50+ tasks (retrieval, classification, clustering, reranking, STS) for comparing text embedding models on a standardized leaderboard.

title: Needle in a Haystack
existing_short_definition: A long-context evaluation that buries a specific fact (the needle) at a random position in a huge irrelevant context (the haystack) and asks the model to retrieve it.

title: Open LLM Leaderboard
existing_short_definition: Hugging Face's leaderboard that ranks open-weights LLMs on a standardized benchmark suite — the most-consulted reference for 'what open model should I try?'

title: OpenTelemetry (GenAI / OpenInference)
existing_short_definition: The emerging standard for tracing LLM applications — OpenTelemetry semantic conventions plus vendor-specific attributes like prompts, tokens, and cost.

title: Pipeline (LLM Serving Pipeline)
existing_short_definition: The sequence of components (tokenizer, model, sampler, post-processors) packaged together as a single runnable unit behind a common API.

title: Portkey
existing_short_definition: An LLM gateway platform with multi-provider routing, fallbacks, caching, guardrails, and observability — targeted at enterprise LLM infrastructure.

title: PromptFoo
existing_short_definition: An open-source CLI and framework for testing, evaluating, and red-teaming LLM prompts — think Jest for LLMs.

title: RAGAS (RAG Assessment)
existing_short_definition: An open-source RAG evaluation framework that scores faithfulness, answer relevance, context precision, and context recall without human ground truth.

title: Shadow Deployment
existing_short_definition: Running a new model alongside production, processing live traffic in parallel but not serving its outputs to users — for safe quality comparison.

title: SWE-Agent / Agentless
existing_short_definition: Agent frameworks designed to maximize SWE-Bench performance — SWE-Agent via custom agent-computer interface, Agentless via simpler structured pipelines.

title: SWE-Bench
existing_short_definition: A benchmark where an LLM must resolve real-world GitHub issues in open-source Python repos by producing a patch that passes the repo's tests.

title: Synthetic Data
existing_short_definition: Training data generated by an LLM instead of collected from humans — cheap at scale but prone to introducing the generator's biases and failure modes.

title: Tau-Bench (τ-Bench)
existing_short_definition: A benchmark from Sierra that evaluates LLM agents on realistic customer-support tasks over APIs with a simulated user and success criteria.

title: Time to First Token (TTFT)
existing_short_definition: The latency between sending a request and receiving the first generated token — dominated by prefill time, critical for perceived responsiveness.

title: Token Per Second (TPS)
existing_short_definition: A throughput metric for LLM inference measuring generated tokens per second — the single most common latency headline number.

title: Weights & Biases (W&B)
existing_short_definition: A managed platform for experiment tracking, model monitoring, and LLM evaluation widely used across ML research and industry.
